customer_audiences.description: Audiences are the preferred method of segmenting customers for high level analytics reporting.
customer_audiences.uses: Use YOUR_SCHEMA.DIM_CUSTOMER for customer-level analysis. Use YOUR_SCHEMA.DIM_USER for location-level analysis.

customer_sub_audiences.description: Sub Audiences are granular definitions of Audiences used for more granular reporting. Multiple sub audiences roll up to a single audience.
customer_sub_audiences.uses: Use for more granular level customer or location analysis.

volume_segments.description: Customer segmentation based on 91-day rolling Gross Payment Volume (GPV) calculated in USD. Categorizes customers by payment processing volume.

values.Inactive: Customers with no payment processing activity ($0 GPV).
values.Micro: Small-scale customers processing $0.01 to $125,000 in 91-day GPV.
values.SMB: Small and Medium Business customers processing $125,000.01 to $500,000 in 91-day GPV.
values.Mid-Market: Mid-market customers processing $500,000.01 to $10,000,000 in 91-day GPV.
values.Enterprise: Enterprise-level customers processing over $10,000,000 in 91-day GPV.

uses: Customer targeting for product upselling and cross-selling campaigns
Customer support routing and service level assignment
Customer retention analysis and churn prediction modeling
Revenue forecasting and growth projections by segment

key_fields.customer_segment: Primary segment classification (Micro, SMB, Mid-Market, Enterprise, Inactive)
key_fields.customer_sub_segment: Granular sub-segment for detailed targeting (e.g., Micro-Low, SMB-High, Ent-Mid)

important_notes: Filter by effective_end = '9999-12-31' for current active segment assignments
GPV thresholds are measured in gross base units and converted to USD

source_user_type.description: The source where the user (customer or location) signed up. Only use if the user asks for sign up related questions.
